channel.title: Blue Cross VT News Mentions
channel.description: Mentions of BCBSVT, Blue Cross VT, and Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont from Vermont news outlets.

item.title: Vermont Business Magazine: Vermont gets $7.62 million in Northern Border Regional Commission grants
item.description:
Source: Vermont Business Magazine

Matched terms: Health care

Vermont is receiving $7.62 million in Northern Border Regional Commission grants to support projects including modernizing healthcare facilities.

Why included: Grant funding for healthcare facilities in Vermont.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Health Insurance Search: Major Medicare Advantage insurers appear to deny care for profit, federal watchdog finds - Healthcare Dive
item.description:
Source: Google News Health Insurance Search

Matched terms: Medicare Advantage, Medicare, Health insurance, Health care

A federal watchdog report indicates that major Medicare Advantage insurers may be denying care for profit.

Why included: Medicare Advantage insurer practices and potential profit motive.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Health Insurance Search: ADA urges dental-specific approach in CMS interoperability, prior authorization proposal - American Dental Association
item.description:
Source: Google News Health Insurance Search

Matched terms: Prior authorization & claims

The American Dental Association is advocating for a dental-specific approach to CMS interoperability and prior authorization proposals.

Why included: Prior authorization and interoperability policy for dental care.

Read the original story

item.title: MyChamplainValley: Massive measles outbreak in Virginia county continues to grow, nearly 90 cases reported
item.description:
Source: MyChamplainValley

Matched terms: Vaccines

A significant measles outbreak in Virginia has reached nearly 90 cases, prompting public health officials to advise caution for the sick and unvaccinated.

Why included: Public health outbreak and vaccination concerns.

Read the original story

Note: the full article text could not be fetched; matching used the feed text only.

item.title: Google News Health Insurance Search: House committee votes to halt prior authorization pilot impacting radiology services - Radiology Business
item.description:

item.title: Vermont Public: State regulators approve new surgery center for the Upper Valley
item.description:
Source: Vermont Public

Matched terms: Hospitals

State regulators have approved a new surgery center for the Upper Valley, which is expected to reduce wait times and offer a cheaper alternative to hospital care.

Why included: New surgery center impacts hospital competition and costs.

BlueCross BlueShield of Vermont is proposing a cheaper plan to address a decline in insurance enrollment.

Why included: Names BCBSVT directly, addresses coverage affordability.

Source: Google News Health Insurance Search

Why included: Industry consolidation affects health care costs and access

item.title: Google News Search: Vermont Blue Advantage will not sell plans at all in 2026 - VTDigger
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Vermont Blue Advantage, Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont, Blue Cross

Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont announced that Vermont Blue Advantage will stop offering Medicare Advantage plans in the state for 2026, affecting 26,000 people.

Why included: Names BCBSVT and Vermont Blue Advantage ending Medicare plans.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: These are the health insurers that quit Medicare Advantage - Modern Healthcare
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross

This article lists health insurers that have stopped offering Medicare Advantage plans.

Why included: Mentions 'Blue Cross' in the context of Medicare Advantage plans.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Blue Cross Blue Shield to drop Medicare Advantage plans by year’s end - WCAX
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross, Vermont Blue Advantage

Blue Cross Blue Shield will discontinue Vermont Blue Advantage Medicare Advantage plans next year due to significant financial losses, exceeding $62 million last year.

Why included: Names BCBSVT and Vermont Blue Advantage ending Medicare plans due to losses.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Vermont Blue Advantage to end Medicare plans - MyChamplainValley.com
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Vermont Blue Advantage

Vermont Blue Advantage will cease offering its Medicare plans.

Why included: Names Vermont Blue Advantage ending Medicare plans.

item.title: Google News Search: Tens of thousands Vermonters are set to lose Medicare Advantage option in 2026 - VTDigger
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont, Vermont Blue Advantage, Blue Cross

Tens of thousands of Vermonters will lose their Medicare Advantage options in 2026 as providers like UnitedHealthcare and Vermont Blue Advantage discontinue coverage.

Why included: Mentions BCBSVT's Vermont Blue Advantage ending Medicare plans.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Vermont Blue Advantage to end Medicare Advantage plans this year - WPTZ
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Vermont Blue Advantage

Vermont Blue Advantage will end all Medicare Advantage plans by the end of the calendar year.

Why included: Names Vermont Blue Advantage ending Medicare plans.

Read the original story

item.title: Google News Search: Blue Cross VT names Beth-Ann Roberts as next president and CEO - Vermont Business Magazine
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross VT, Blue Cross, Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont

Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont has named Beth-Ann Roberts as its next president and CEO, effective January 10, 2026, with a transition period starting November 10.

Why included: Names BCBSVT and announces new leadership.

Read the original story

item.title: Bennington Banner: Green Mountain Camp launches the Sarah Bradley and Edith Bradley Memorial Endowment
item.description:

A memorial endowment has been launched for a summer camp founded by women inspired by public health data.

Why included: Mentions public health inspiration

item.title: Google News Search: Vermont regulators approve a trimmed increase to 2026 health insurance premiums for large employers - VTDigger
item.category: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont
item.description:
Source: Google News Search

Matched terms: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Vermont, Blue Cross

Vermont regulators have approved a 13.7% average increase to health insurance premiums for large employers charged by Blue Cross Blue Shield of Vermont.

Why included: Names BCBSVT directly, legislative action on coverage

Read the original story

item.title: Bennington Banner: Sanders calls for investigation into vaccine committee firings
item.description:
Source: Bennington Banner

Matched terms: Vaccines

Senator Bernie Sanders is calling for an investigation into the firing of members of a vaccine committee.

Why included: Vaccine policy and public health

Read the original story

item.title: VTDigger Facebook: VTDigger Facebook post: BlueCross BlueShield wants to offer a new suite of cheaper plans, but the state’
item.description:
Source: VTDigger Facebook

Matched terms: Blue Cross, Health care

BlueCross BlueShield wants to offer cheaper plans, but the state's healthcare regulator doubts they will yield real savings.

Why included: Names BCBSVT directly, regulatory action on coverage

Comments:

  • Janet Fitzpatrick: Allow people over 30 to buy Blue Cross Blue Shield catastrophic plans! Those plans are much more affordable.
  • Mark Byland: sp/healthcare/healthprofit
  • Kristin Bryn Reid: The health insurance system in Vermont is voing exactly as designed for the last 20 or so years We tried to warn you
  • Dylan Greer: Or hear me out… we pay a boat load of money to insurance companies… they can pay for our healthcare
  • Janice Wright: Cheaper plan = lower monthly payments, but higher deductibles, higher co-pays, less coverage, and fewer in-network doctors
  • Jay Files: My work just switched to MVP gold great insurance we had blue cross it covers almost nothing worthless insurance you have to fight with them for everything
  • Amy Beth: "higher" deductible plan my deductible is already $9500 so unless they are significantly dropping the monthly premium which is between $809-900 a month for one person this isn't helpful. Health insurance for those of us who have to purchase it from the marketplace is ridiculous.
  • Ruth Miller: I dunno...I just got a class action suit settlement payment from BCBS. I'm done with them, especially after leaving me with no gap coverage after they pulled Blue Advantage out of VT. They seem to lose money on a continuing basis.
  • Beth Stevens: My plan for myself a child was supposed to be 650 a month with a 5k deductible, got my bill over 900 a month. I canceled as I was still paying for meds and co pay
  • Matthew Funk: It’s almost like y’all already Fcked it up

Read the original story
